records.ety: [L. liberatus, p. p. of liberare to free, fr. liber free. See Liberal, a., and cf. Deliver.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Liberated (-ā`td); p. pr. & vb. n. Liberating (-ā`tng).]

defs: To release from restraint or bondage; to set at liberty; to free; to manumit; to disengage; as, to liberate a slave or prisoner; to liberate the mind from prejudice; to liberate gases.
